gaps: The bug is about a web portal (lens.poly.com) — specifically the Manage > Policies > Individual Device list not showing the Voyager Free 60+. All 15 existing test cases are exclusively about the Poly Studio Mobile Android app (home screen, device list, connection status, UI elements). None test the lens.poly.com web portal at all.

The bug involves device policy management (policy assignment/removal) on a web-based device management platform. None of the existing test cases cover policy management, device inventory synchronization, or the relationship between Inventory and Individual Device policy lists.

The bug is specific to the Voyager Free 60+ model not appearing in a particular web portal list. No existing test case validates that specific device models appear correctly in policy assignment lists on lens.poly.com.

The bug involves a cross-system discrepancy: device appears in Inventory but not in the Individual Device policy list. No existing test covers this type of data consistency check between different sections of a web management portal.

The bug requires testing the lens.poly.com web portal's device listing behavior for policy management — a completely different platform (web) and domain (enterprise device management) than the mobile app tests.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-001: Verify Voyager Free 60+ appears in Inventory on lens.poly.com after device sync
steps: Log in to lens.poly.com as an admin user with device management privileges.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory.
Locate the search/filter bar and enter 'Voyager Free 60+'.
Verify the Voyager Free 60+ device appears in the Inventory list with correct serial number, model name, and status.
Note the serial number and device ID shown in Inventory.
generated_testcases.expected: Voyager Free 60+ is listed in the Inventory section with correct model name, serial number, and connection status.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-002: Verify Voyager Free 60+ appears in Manage > Policies > Individual Device list
steps: Log in to lens.poly.com as an admin user.
Navigate to Manage → Policies.
Select an existing policy or create a new one (e.g., 'Test Policy - VF60+').
Click on the policy to open its details.
Navigate to the 'Individual Device' tab/section within the policy.
In the device assignment field, search for 'Voyager Free 60+'.
Check if the device appears in the dropdown/list of assignable devices.
If the device appears, select it and assign it to the policy.
Save the policy changes.
generated_testcases.expected: Voyager Free 60+ appears in the Individual Device list within the policy assignment section. The device can be selected and assigned to the policy successfully.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-003: Cross-section consistency — Voyager Free 60+ in Inventory vs. Individual Device policy list
steps: Log in to lens.poly.com as an admin user.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory.
Search for 'Voyager Free 60+' and confirm the device is listed. Note its serial number and device ID.
Navigate to Manage → Policies.
Open any existing policy (or create a new one) and go to the Individual Device tab.
Search for the same Voyager Free 60+ device using its serial number or device ID noted from Inventory.
Check if the device appears in the Individual Device assignment list.
If the device does NOT appear, attempt to refresh the page, log out and log back in, and re-check.
If the device still does not appear, this is a cross-section data inconsistency bug.
generated_testcases.expected: The Voyager Free 60+ device that appears in Manage → Inventory should also appear in Manage → Policies → [Policy Name] → Individual Device list. Both sections should show consistent device data for the same serial number.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-004: Verify Voyager Free 60+ policy removal via Individual Device list
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is already assigned to a policy on lens.poly.com.
Log in to lens.poly.com as an admin user.
Navigate to Manage → Policies.
Select the policy that the Voyager Free 60+ is assigned to.
Go to the Individual Device tab.
Locate the Voyager Free 60+ in the assigned devices list.
Click the remove/delete icon next to the Voyager Free 60+ entry.
Confirm the removal in any dialog that appears.
Save the policy changes.
Refresh the page and navigate back to the same policy's Individual Device tab.
Verify the Voyager Free 60+ is no longer listed as an assigned device.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory and verify the device still exists in Inventory (policy should be removed but device remains).
generated_testcases.expected: Voyager Free 60+ is successfully removed from the policy's Individual Device list. The device remains in Inventory but shows no policy assignment. The removal persists after page refresh.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-005: Verify Voyager Free 60+ appears in Individual Device list after device re-sync from Poly Studio Mobile app
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is paired and connected to the Poly Studio Mobile Android app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the connected Android device.
Navigate to the device settings screen for Voyager Free 60+.
Trigger a manual sync/refresh (e.g., pull-to-refresh or tap Sync button if available).
On lens.poly.com web portal, log in as admin.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory and confirm the Voyager Free 60+ is listed with updated last-sync timestamp.
Navigate to Manage → Policies → [Any Policy] → Individual Device tab.
Search for the Voyager Free 60+ device.
Verify the device appears in the Individual Device assignment list.
generated_testcases.expected: After a manual sync from the Poly Studio Mobile app, the Voyager Free 60+ appears in both Inventory and the Individual Device policy list on lens.poly.com with an updated last-sync timestamp.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-006: Verify Voyager Free 60+ appears in Individual Device list after Bluetooth reconnect and app relaunch
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is paired with the Android device running Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and confirm Voyager Free 60+ is connected.
Disconnect Bluetooth from the Android device's Bluetooth settings.
Wait 10 seconds, then reconnect Bluetooth to the Voyager Free 60+.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and confirm the device reconnects successfully.
Navigate to device settings and trigger a sync if available.
On lens.poly.com, log in as admin.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory and verify the Voyager Free 60+ appears with recent sync time.
Navigate to Manage → Policies → [Any Policy] → Individual Device tab.
Search for the Voyager Free 60+ and verify it appears in the assignable devices list.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect and app relaunch, the Voyager Free 60+ syncs to lens.poly.com and appears in both Inventory and the Individual Device policy list.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-007: Verify Voyager Free 60+ appears in Individual Device list after phone reboot
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is paired with the Android device running Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and confirm Voyager Free 60+ is connected.
Reboot the Android phone.
After reboot, open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to automatically reconnect to Voyager Free 60+ (or manually reconnect via Bluetooth settings).
Confirm the device is connected in the app.
Navigate to device settings and trigger a sync if available.
On lens.poly.com, log in as admin.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory and verify the Voyager Free 60+ appears with recent sync time.
Navigate to Manage → Policies → [Any Policy] → Individual Device tab.
Search for the Voyager Free 60+ and verify it appears in the assignable devices list.
generated_testcases.expected: After a phone reboot and app relaunch, the Voyager Free 60+ syncs to lens.poly.com and appears in both Inventory and the Individual Device policy list.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-008: Verify Voyager Free 60+ appears in Individual Device list after app force-kill and relaunch
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is paired and connected to the Poly Studio Mobile Android app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and confirm Voyager Free 60+ is connected.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to reconnect to Voyager Free 60+.
Confirm the device is connected in the app.
Navigate to device settings and trigger a sync if available.
On lens.poly.com, log in as admin.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory and verify the Voyager Free 60+ appears with recent sync time.
Navigate to Manage → Policies → [Any Policy] → Individual Device tab.
Search for the Voyager Free 60+ and verify it appears in the assignable devices list.
generated_testcases.expected: After force-killing and relaunching the Poly Studio Mobile app, the Voyager Free 60+ syncs to lens.poly.com and appears in both Inventory and the Individual Device policy list.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-009: Verify Voyager Free 20 also appears correctly in Individual Device list (sibling model comparison)
steps: Precondition: Both Voyager Free 60+ and Voyager Free 20 are paired and synced to lens.poly.com.
Log in to lens.poly.com as an admin user.
Navigate to Manage → Inventory.
Search for 'Voyager Free 20' and confirm it is listed with correct model name and serial number.
Search for 'Voyager Free 60+' and confirm it is also listed.
Navigate to Manage → Policies → [Any Policy] → Individual Device tab.
Search for 'Voyager Free 20' and verify it appears in the assignable devices list.
Search for 'Voyager Free 60+' and verify it also appears in the assignable devices list.
Compare: both sibling models should behave consistently in the Individual Device list.
generated_testcases.expected: Both Voyager Free 20 and Voyager Free 60+ appear in Inventory and in the Individual Device policy list on lens.poly.com. There is no model-specific exclusion for either device.

generated_testcases.title: LENS-010: Verify policy assignment persists for Voyager Free 60+ across browser sessions
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ is assigned to a policy via lens.poly.com.
Log in to lens.poly.com as an admin user.
Navigate to Manage → Policies → [Policy with VF60+ assigned] → Individual Device tab.
Confirm the Voyager Free 60+ is listed as an assigned device.
Log out of lens.poly.com.
Close the browser completely.
Open a new browser session and log in to lens.poly.com again.
Navigate to Manage → Policies → [Same Policy] → Individual Device tab.
Verify the Voyager Free 60+ is still listed as an assigned device.
generated_testcases.expected: The Voyager Free 60+ policy assignment persists across browser sessions. After logging out, closing the browser, and logging back in, the device still appears in the policy's Individual Device list.
